2. National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP)  Vision, Mission & Objectives
Chunk ID: msajce_incubation_chunk_02 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/Incubation&Startup.php;#tab2 | ~480 tokens
Context: Covers the NISP vision and mission statements and both short-term and long-term objectives for promoting innovation and
entrepreneurship at MSAJCE.
National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP)  MSAJCE
Vision:
To cater the needs of student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas thereby introducing a culture of entrepreneurship inside
campus which will strengthen our education system and thereby promoting national economic and social growth.
Mission:
To develop an ecosystem with required infrastructure that can enable students and faculty to innovate and prototype their
potential ideas with industrial standards and support from Government, industry and reputed academic institutions around the
world and help them to realize their potentials.
Short Term Objectives:
1. To help student groups to prototype their ideas.
2. To improve innovation, creative and design thinking among student community.
3. Incubation facility for faculty driven start-up and student/alumni start-up.
4. Organize FDP, seminars and workshops, distinguish talks for students, faculty and alumni and promote entrepreneurial
culture.
5. Strengthen institute-industry interaction cell activity and effectively use the outcomes for achieving the mission.
Long Term Objectives:
1. Associate with DST, CII, MSME and other academic institutions for transferring world class facility for MSAJCE IEDC
activities.
2. Improve quality of research work among students and to attain patent, which can be commercially used in production.
3. Provide a platform for students to develop innovative products with global recognition and generate business opportunities.
4. Generate revenues through consultancy work and student start-ups.
5. Spread awareness to students and faculty regarding IPR related activities.
6. Strategic partnership linkage with:
a. Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India (EDII), Chennai.
b. National Innovation Foundation (NIF) of India to submit ideas and apply for schemes.
c. Procure fund from AICTE for Entrepreneurship Development Cell.

4. KPI Monitor & Evaluation  Innovation Outcomes and Activities
Chunk ID: msajce_incubation_chunk_04 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/Incubation&Startup.php;#tab2 | ~510 tokens
Context: Detailed KPI framework for MSAJCE's innovation and startup policy covering vision goals, outcomes, and planned activities
with verification mechanisms.
KPI Monitor & Evaluation:
Hierarchy | Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) | Verification
Vision | Increase in Self-Employment Rate; 5 Established Start-ups per year | NIRF Rankings
Goal | Establish Environment with multiple levels of support for innovation & Entrepreneurship and startups; Students will choose
Entrepreneurship as career | ARIIA Rankings
Outcomes | All students & faculty with entrepreneurship orientation; Students & faculty motivated to start entrepreneurial activity;
5 IPR/Innovations developed ideas/year; 5 Student/Early-Stage Start-ups to be formed/year; In-house Expert Capacity facility
available for Advisory Services; 25% of Student & faculty mass exposed to awareness/orientation programs; Students covered
through entrepreneurship Education via MOOC, Classroom, Experiential Learning; 250 beneficiaries accessing infrastructure &
facilities per day/month/year; 25% innovators identified, 25 awarded/recognized, 25 supported; 5% Student projects turn to
commercialised innovations; 5 IPR-based product/services generated and registration filed; In-house trained professional
developed for advisory services; 5 Research Studies on Entrepreneurship published; 2 Regional/National/International linkages
established for start-up & innovation; 5% Representatives of experts & entrepreneurial students across Dept. & Disciplines; 5
Beneficiaries referred to Incubators/investors; 10 Beneficiaries generated under various schemes | Quarterly Newsletter,
Biannual Survey, Monthly Progress Report
Activities | 5 Education/Skill certification programs on Entrepreneurship, IPR, Innovation; 25 workshops, awareness, market
outreach events; 12 networking events (Intra and Inter-institutional) organized; 5 skill and competency development training
programs/FDPs/EDPs; 5 research studies on Entrepreneurship; 1 national/regional award and campus Hackathon; Incentivizing
Entrepreneurship with policies, toolkits, Start-up Manual; 1% of total budget/year against total institution revenue for start-up |
Biannual Survey, Quarterly Newsletter, Monthly Progress Report, Review Meetings

6. Institution Innovation Council (IIC)  About, Vision, Mission & Office Bearers
Chunk ID: msajce_incubation_chunk_06 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/Incubation&Startup.php;#tab4 | ~505 tokens
Context: Covers the IIC constitution, its major functions, vision, mission, and office bearer details for MSAJCE's Institution Innovation
Council.
Institution Innovation Council (IIC)  MSAJCE
IIC-MSAJCE has been constituted as per guidelines of the Ministry of Human Resource Development Innovation Cell (MIC).
Introduction:
Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD), Govt. of India has established 'MHRD's Innovation Cell (MIC)' to
systematically foster the culture of innovation amongst all Higher Education Institutions (HEIs). The primary mandate of MIC is to
encourage, inspire and nurture young students by supporting them to work with new ideas and transform them into prototypes
while they are in their formative years.
MIC has envisioned encouraging creation of 'Institution's Innovation Councils (IICs)' across selected HEIs. A network of IICs is
established to promote innovation in institutions through multitudinous modes leading to an innovation promotion eco-system in
campuses.
Major Focus of IIC:
- To create a vibrant local innovation ecosystem.
- Start-up supporting mechanism in HEIs.
- Prepare institute for Atal Ranking of Institutions on Innovation Achievements (ARIIA) Framework.
- Establish Function at Ecosystem for Scouting Ideas and Pre-incubation of Ideas.
- Develop better Cognitive Ability for Technology Students.
Functions of IIC:
- Identify and reward innovations and share success stories.
- Organize periodic workshops/seminars/interactions with entrepreneurs, investors, professionals and create a mentor pool for
student innovators.
- Network with peers and national entrepreneurship development organizations.
- Create an Institution's Innovation Portal to highlight innovative projects by institution's faculty and students.
- Organize Hackathons, idea competitions, mini-challenges with industry involvement.
